stem: A GPS navigation navigation system uses 3 parallel satellite links for redundancy. The position fixed requires at least one satellite link to function. satellite link 1 has a reliability of 77%. satellite link 2 has a reliability of 93%. satellite link 3 has a reliability between 69% and 86% (uncertain due to limited operational data). What is the overall probability that the position fixed?

ground_truth.answer: P(position fixed) ranges from 0.995 to 0.9977. Point estimate: 0.9963.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 satellite links fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.23 × 0.07 × 0.31 = 0.005. P(works) = 0.995.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.23 × 0.07 × 0.14 = 0.0023. P(works) = 0.9977.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
